response.justification: Bogdan has exceptional geospatial domain expertise (GISCollective founder, GIS applications, FOSS4G speaker) and solid distributed-systems experience from his microservices work at Dunnhumby. However, he presents a significant technical risk: his C++ experience is dated (10+ years old, minimal) and his last four years are entirely in TypeScript/Node.js web development—moving away from systems programming rather than toward it. While his demonstrated ability to learn new tech is valuable, ramping back into modern C++ for a primary-language role is uncertain. The geospatial domain fit is rare enough to warrant exploration, but the C++ gap requires careful assessment during an interview.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise: GISCollective founder, open-source GIS contributions, proven production experience with mapping platforms
Large-scale distributed-systems experience: microservices infrastructure handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby; proven Docker/Kubernetes infrastructure work
Strong software craftsmanship foundation: TDD advocate, testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), demonstrated ability to learn and master multiple tech stacks across web, mobile, embedded
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and outdated (10+ years), with recent trajectory firmly in TypeScript/web development; unclear ability to ramp back to modern C++ as a primary language
No Rust experience mentioned, and no evidence of systems-level expertise in fault tolerance, consistency models, or service discovery patterns needed for routing pipelines
No demonstrated experience with navigation/routing systems or algorithms; geospatial work is primarily data collection/mapping platforms, not navigation guidance or routing optimization

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain knowledge and production experience
Distributed systems and large-scale infrastructure experience
